CheckTitle: AI Agent Identity Must Follow Organisational Naming Convention
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Identity**. Validates that the agent's cloud identity (IAM role, managed identity, service account) adheres to the organisation's naming standard. A predictable naming scheme enables automated policy scoping (e.g., SCPs, Azure Policy) and simplifies incident investigation.
Risk: Non-compliant names can circumvent name-based IAM policies or SCPs, allowing the agent to bypass intended permission boundaries. They also make it harder to identify rogue or shadow-AI deployments during security reviews.

Code.Other:
1. Rename the IAM role / service account to match the agreed naming pattern (e.g., agent-<team>-<purpose>-<env>).
2. Update all trust policies, permission boundaries, and code references to the new name.
3. Enforce naming via a CI/CD policy gate or IaC linting rule.

Recommendation.Text: Define and enforce an agent identity naming standard through IaC templates and CI/CD gates. Validate names before deployment.

Notes: ASPM-002 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
